steps.title: Connect captivity to influence
steps.content: The passage compares two primate studies: a captive group showed more right-handed individuals, while a wild chimpanzee group did not show that tendency. The final sentence then gives the relevant difference between the groups: captive animals spend much more time with humans than wild animals do.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported explanations
steps.content: Choice A invents a difference in how the studies measured handedness. Choices C and D both invent sample-size problems that the passage never discusses. The supported completion should link the captive animals' extended contact with humans to the right-handedness found in the captive-primate study. The answer is B.
